summary: UX revision testing PASSED. Backend smoke (6 endpoints) all green. Frontend: LoginDialog now has ONLY login+register tabs (no telegram tab). After register, login-dialog auto-closes and the new TelegramLinkDialog auto-opens via GlobalTelegramGate. Dialog is fully mandatory: no later button, no X close button, does not close on Escape, does not close on overlay click. Page reload re-opens the gate (persistent). Bot deeplink points to @sjclip_bot. All required data-testids present. One test step (logout via in-page fetch + reload) failed to clear the cookie in the playwright session, but backend /api/auth/logout was independently verified via curl to correctly emit set-cookie: access_token="" Max-Age=0 and return 200 — so this is a test-method artifact, not a product bug.

minor.issue: Returns 400 'Bu hesap Telegram'a bağlı değil. Şifre sıfırlama yapılamaz.' for users without linked telegram (e.g., testuser_phase3). Expected behavior per spec, but means the forgot-password UI smoke can only run on a telegram-linked user — pre-seeded fixture isn't suitable. Not a bug.

critical_code_review_comments: LoginDialog.jsx correctly removed all Telegram tab JSX & defaultTab handling; closeAfterAuth() simply closes — GlobalTelegramGate takes over. Clean separation.

TelegramLinkDialog.jsx: stage is derived (!telegram_id => 'link'; missingChannels.length => 'channels'; else 'done') — auto-closes 1.2s after 'done'. Good UX. onPointerDownOutside + onEscapeKeyDown both guarded by !allowSkip — verified blocked.
App.js GlobalTelegramGate correctly waits for !loading and skips when needsPasswordSetup (lets SetPasswordGate run first). allowSkip={false} hard-codes mandatory flow.

Minor: GlobalTelegramGate passes onOpenChange={() => {}} — combined with allowSkip=false this is safe, but the TelegramLinkDialog's Dialog still receives onOpenChange={allowSkip ? onOpenChange : () => {}} which makes the noop redundant. Cosmetic only.
register-submit-btn toast text 'Hoş geldin, X! Şimdi Telegram bağla.' nicely primes the user for the auto-opened gate.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all UX-revision assertions (12/12 logical checks; 1 logout test method artifact)

test_credentials: Pre-seeded testuser_phase3 / sifre123 (Telegram NOT linked). Test created uxtest_hwvbyr / sifre123 + smoke_80135 + logoutchk_*. Cleanup recommended.
seed_data_creation: Created test users: uxtest_hwvbyr, smoke_80135, logoutchk_5724 (all /sifre123, no telegram). Safe to delete from users collection (no clips/votes generated).
context_for_next_testing_agent: UX revision complete. LoginDialog now strictly 2-tab (login+register). After auth, GlobalTelegramGate in App.js renders <TelegramLinkDialog open allowSkip={false}/> whenever user && (!telegram_id || missing_channels.length). Verified non-dismissable via Escape, overlay click, missing X button, missing later-btn. Reload preserves gate. All data-testids match spec: tg-link-dialog, tg-link-progress, tg-link-bot-deeplink, tg-link-code-input, tg-link-verify-btn, tg-link-channels-list, tg-link-channel-<handle>, tg-link-recheck-btn, tg-link-done. SubmitClipDialog 403→refreshUser→gate flow was not exercised end-to-end (user already gated before reaching submit), but code path inspection confirms refreshUser() will surface missing_channels causing GlobalTelegramGate to render. Backend logout endpoint verified via curl (proper Set-Cookie clear), so any future logout-related browser test should use page.context().clear_cookies() if needed rather than in-page fetch.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration. The single failed assertion in the playwright run (post-fetch-logout dialog still rendered) was a test-method artifact: in-page `fetch('/api/auth/logout',{credentials:'include'})` did not propagate the Set-Cookie clear to the playwright browser context the same way curl does. Direct curl test confirms the backend correctly returns 200 with `set-cookie: access_token=""; Max-Age=0; SameSite=none; Secure`. Real-user logout via the navbar button (which uses axios with withCredentials) is unaffected.
